title: mcptest session ledger (v1)
description: One record of the mcptest session ledger: a structured, append-only run record of MCP tool calls keyed to a session, so behavioral eval can assert what an agent did at the tool boundary (which tools, in what order, with what params, given what inputs) instead of parsing a transcript. A ledger file is newline-delimited JSON (NDJSON): exactly one `header` record, then one `tool_call` record per call in call order. This schema validates a single record. OSS (mcptest, SBproxy) emits this artifact; the enterprise tier consumes, aggregates, signs, and governs it.

header.title: Ledger header record
header.description: The first line of a ledger file. Carries the session/run metadata every following record is grouped under.

schema_version.description: Ledger schema version this file conforms to, e.g. "v1".

session_id.description: Groups every record produced by one run or user task. Derived from the run id.

run_id.description: The mcptest run id (UUIDv7) the session was derived from, when available.

suite.description: Suite file path or name the run executed.

tool_call.title: Ledger tool-call record
tool_call.description: One MCP tool call as it crossed the boundary. The fields are the structure behavioral eval queries; payloads are redacted before they are written.

session_id.description: Session this call belongs to. Matches the header `session_id`.

agent_id.description: Which agent made the call. Set in multi-agent (planner/worker) runs so a per-agent baseline does not conflate behaviors; null or absent for single-agent runs.

hop_index.description: Zero-based position of this call in the run. Lets a baseline assert a call happened at a specific step, after the agent saw the output of the prior step.

tool_name.description: Bare tool name (without any `<server>__` namespace prefix).

server.description: MCP server the call routed to.

params.description: Structured tool arguments, redacted. Any JSON value, so eval can assert specific argument fields, not just the tool identity.

result.description: Tool result payload, redacted. Any JSON value.

is_error.description: Whether the server flagged the result as an error.

inputs_digest.description: Stable fingerprint of the context the agent had in front of it when it made the call, so a baseline can assert the inputs were identical across runs (a determinism check) without storing the full context.

started_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp the call was dispatched.

duration_ms.description: Round-trip latency of the call in milliseconds.

caller.description: How the call was issued: a direct model tool call, or one emitted from a code-execution sandbox.
